description_long: This sculpture depicts three ancient Egyptian figures carved in high relief from a single block of stone. The central figure is seated and wears a headdress with a sun disk and cow horns, likely representing the goddess Hathor. To the right stands a male figure adorned with a tall, conical crown, possibly a pharaoh, while to the left stands a smaller, more slender figure, possibly a goddess or attendant. The figures are closely grouped, with the seated figure's feet resting on a base inscribed with hieroglyphs. The back of the sculpture is a flat, vertical slab, providing structural support and a backdrop for the figures. The surface is smooth but shows subtle textural details in the clothing, headdresses, and inscriptions.
description: A detailed stone sculpture of three ancient Egyptian figures, including a seated goddess and two standing attendants, with inscribed hieroglyphs on the base.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing all three figures, the seated central goddess, the standing pharaoh, and attendant, with visible hieroglyphs on the base.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the seated figure and the depth of the relief, with the pharaoh's crown and the slab's thickness visible.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the flat, unadorned back slab of the sculpture, with no figures or inscriptions visible.
description_view_3: Right side view displaying the profile of the figures, especially the seated goddess and the standing attendant, and the thickness of the base.
attribute_subject_matter: Ancient Egyptian deities and royalty in high relief
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate a specific setting.
